text: We present a Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) approach to the human pose estimation problem. Instead of processing pose estimation as an iterative or iterative-deep process, we choose to process it as a single, direct regression problem where our network outputs the 2D Cartesian coordinates of body joints at once. A cascade of DNNs regression the joints one after another until a strong detection is located, refines the detection and all its corresponding joints locations, leading to high localization on each joints with great improvement over state of the art, even while the DNNs reason about a more holistic body pose estimation, rather than focusing solely to each parts as most part-based approaches do, resulting in very accurate approximations of the global human body configuration which traditional methods fail to estimate non linear global body shapes due to the local and linear approaches, without having to define hand crafted features and no graphical models definition, therefore achieving the state of the art results in four human pose estimation challenging academic evaluations using four testing data-sets which have a large number of various and natural images.
headline: Fully Human Written
